reasoning: The question asks whether LMT's closing price will increase over a one-week period from 2026-03-29 to 2026-04-05. Since April 5 is a Sunday and April 3 is Good Friday (a likely market holiday), this covers about 4 trading days in early April. The price as of March 27 (which serves as the March 29 close) is 615.84, following a pullback from earlier highs near 670. Weekly stock movements operate largely as a random walk with a slight upward drift over time, yielding historical base-rate probabilities of a one-week increase at approximately 52-54%. The linear model from the fetched time series puts the probability of a higher price at 0.55. Absent any highly predictable major impending catalysts (LMT earnings are typically late April), there is no strong signal to deviate significantly from the base rate for a random walk. Therefore, the estimate remains very close to 50%, with a minimal premium reflecting the equity market's long-term positive drift.
